intro: Saturday morning. You make coffee. It's the first thing you do.

scenarios.title: The Idea Phase
scenarios.subtitle: Where it all starts.
scenarios.prompt: You sit down with your coffee. Your notes app is open. There are three ideas in there that won't stop bugging you.

choices.text: Pick the one that excites you most and start thinking through it.
choices.reaction: This is the one. For now.

choices.text: You can't decide which one. They all seem good and impossible.
One of them keeps pulling you back. That's probably the one.
choices.reaction: That one won't leave you alone.

choices.text: Close the app. Today feels too early to commit to anything.
choices.reaction: The coffee's still warm. No rush.

scenarios.title: The Skills You've Got
scenarios.subtitle: Everyone brings something to the table.
scenarios.prompt: You're thinking through one of those ideas. What you'd actually need to pull it off. And what you already have.

choices.text: I'm good with people. I could sell this, build relationships around it.
choices.reaction: That counts for more than you think.

choices.text: I'm good at making things. Writing, design, building, creating stuff.
I'm good at figuring things out. Research, problem-solving, learning fast.
I'm not sure what I'd offer. I don't feel like I have a marketable skill.

scenarios.title: The Time Reality
scenarios.subtitle: Be honest with yourself.
scenarios.prompt: It's mid-morning now. You're thinking about this seriously. But you've also got work Monday, errands to do, life happening.

choices.text: I've got plenty of time right now. I could go all in for a bit.
A few hours a week. Nights and weekends around my day job.
Not much. But I'm going to find time somehow.
I could make time if I knew this was actually worth it.

scenarios.title: The Money Situation
scenarios.subtitle: What you're working with.
scenarios.prompt: You're getting serious now. You'd need some things to get started. Tools, maybe materials, a domain. You check your bank account.

choices.text: I've got some savings I can put toward this.
I can spend a little. A subscription or two if it's worth it.
Basically nothing. This has to be free or close to it.
I'd rather invest my time than money right now.

scenarios.title: The Visibility Question
scenarios.subtitle: Putting yourself out there.
scenarios.prompt: It's Sunday now. You've spent the whole weekend thinking about this. Now you're imagining what it would look like to actually tell people about it.

choices.text: Post about it. Start building an audience.
Tell a few people I trust first.
Keep it totally private until it's ready.
I hate the idea of promoting myself.
choices.reaction: You're not alone on that one.

scenarios.title: The Learning Curve
scenarios.subtitle: There's always something you don't know yet.
scenarios.prompt: You're a week into this now. You hit something you don't know how to do. Google doesn't have a clear answer.

choices.text: Find a course or tutorial and dig in.
Figure it out as I go. Learn what I need when I need it.
choices.reaction: Learn by doing. The hard way is the fast way sometimes.

choices.text: Reach out to someone who knows. Ask for help.
Feel overwhelmed and wonder if I'm equipped for this.

scenarios.title: The First Show
scenarios.subtitle: It's real now.
scenarios.prompt: You've got something that actually works now. You show one person you trust. They ask: "So are you going to do anything with this?"

choices.text: I've already thought about how I'd sell it.
I want to see if other people want it first.
choices.reaction: Smart. Test before you build the machine.

choices.text: I have no idea. I just wanted to build it.
choices.reaction: The fun part's over. Now it gets real.

choices.text: I'm terrified to actually put it out there.
choices.reaction: That means it matters to you.

scenarios.title: The Big Picture
scenarios.subtitle: What's this all for?
scenarios.prompt: A few weeks have gone by. You're sitting with what you've built. It's real. People want it. Now you're thinking about what comes next.

choices.text: I want to do this full-time eventually. Be my own boss.
Keep it as a side income. Don't need to quit my job.
I just want to do work I actually care about. Money's secondary.
I want more control over my life. This job isn't cutting it anymore.
